The Foundation

Why Aquaculture Nutrition Matters

Aquaculture accounts for more than half of the world's seafood supply. The productivity and health of farmed shrimp, fish, and other aquatic species depend directly on the quality and balance of the nutrients they receive throughout their life cycle.

Poor nutrition leads to slow growth, weakened immunity, increased disease susceptibility, and high feed conversion ratios — all of which directly impact profitability and sustainability. At Innovare Biopharma, we develop targeted nutritional solutions that address these challenges at every production stage.

✓
Optimised FCR (Feed Conversion Ratio) through precision mineral and vitamin blends
✓
Enhanced immunity reducing disease-related mortality
✓
Healthy pond ecology via water & soil probiotic management
✓
Faster, uniform growth cycles from larval to harvest stage
🦠
Gut Microbiome Health
A balanced gut microbiome improves digestion, nutrient absorption and natural immunity — reducing antibiotic dependency on farms.
🧬
Mineral & Vitamin Balance
Deficiencies in calcium, phosphorus, magnesium or vitamin C impair moulting, bone formation and stress response in shrimp and fish.
💧
Water Quality & Pond Ecology
Ammonia and H₂S build-up from overfeeding and poor digestion create toxic environments. Probiotic pond treatments restore healthy chemistry.
Life-Stage Nutrition

The Right Nutrition at Every Stage

Shrimp and fish have different nutritional needs at each production stage. Our product range is designed to cover every phase from hatchery to harvest.

🥚
Day 0–15
Hatchery Stage
Primary Focus
Larval nutrition & pond preparation

Optimal water chemistry and mineral balance are critical from day one. Vitamin C supplementation supports stress resistance, while pond probiotics establish a healthy microbial baseline.

Our Product Categories

Six Pillars of Aquaculture Nutrition

Every Innovare Biopharma product is developed with a specific nutritional or health function in mind — building a complete system for farm success.

🪨
Pond Minerals
Calcium — shell & exoskeleton formation
Phosphorus — energy metabolism & bone structure
Magnesium — enzyme activation & osmoregulation
Potassium — muscle contraction & ionic balance

Why Choose Us

The Innovare Biopharma Advantage

🔬

Science-Backed Formulations

Each product is developed by aquaculture and veterinary science specialists, with field-validated efficacy data from partner farms across Andhra Pradesh and Telangana.

📞

On-Farm Technical Support

Our team of aquaculture technicians provides direct on-farm consultation — from pond preparation through grow-out — ensuring protocols are correctly implemented.

🌿

Natural & Safe Ingredients

Innovare Biopharma prioritises natural, non-antibiotic solutions: probiotics, phytogenics, and mineral supplements that improve outcomes without chemical residue risks.

📦

Full Product Ecosystem

From pond minerals and feed premixes to gut probiotics and oxygen granules, we provide a complete, integrated nutrition and health system for every farm type.

Technical FAQs

Common Nutrition
Questions Answered

Science-backed answers to the most common aquaculture nutrition questions from our partner farms.

🐟
What is the optimal protein-to-energy ratio for farmed salmon at different life stages?
+
Protein-to-energy (P/E) ratios shift significantly across life stages. Fry and smolts require a higher P/E (~21–23 mg protein/kcal) to support rapid tissue growth, while larger harvest-size fish perform better at lower P/E ratios (~18–20 mg protein/kcal) as dietary energy is increasingly partitioned toward lipid deposition. Over-supplying protein beyond requirement elevates ammonia excretion and feed costs without improving growth.
🌿
How much fishmeal can be replaced with plant-based proteins without impacting performance?
+
Modern diets can reduce fishmeal to 5–15% inclusion while maintaining growth parity, provided anti-nutritional factors such as saponins, phytate, and trypsin inhibitors are adequately processed. Soy protein concentrate, canola meal, and insect meal are leading alternatives. Amino acid balancing — especially lysine, methionine, and threonine — is essential when transitioning away from fishmeal.
⚗️
What role do nucleotides play in aquafeed, and when are they worth including?
+
Nucleotides support intestinal cell proliferation and mucosal integrity, particularly under stress conditions such as first-feeding, disease challenge, or after antibiotic treatment. Their benefit is most pronounced in fast-growing larval and juvenile stages. Typical inclusion rates are 0.1–0.5%, and ROI is highest during health challenges rather than standard grow-out.
💊
How should vitamin and mineral premixes be adjusted for high-oil diets?
+
High dietary lipid levels elevate the requirement for fat-soluble antioxidants — primarily vitamin E and vitamin C — to protect PUFA-rich tissues from lipid peroxidation. Selenium should also be reviewed for its antioxidant synergy with vitamin E. Fat-soluble vitamin bioavailability (A, D, K) is enhanced in high-oil diets, so over-supplementation should be monitored.
📊
How do feed conversion ratios (FCR) vary between species and how should targets be set?
+
FCR benchmarks differ substantially: Atlantic salmon typically achieve 1.0–1.2; rainbow trout 0.9–1.15; shrimp (L. vannamei) 1.5–2.2 due to broadcast feeding losses. Targets should be set relative to species baseline, water temperature, fish size, and feeding technology. Automated feeding systems with feed-waste sensors can reduce FCR by 10–20% versus manual regimes.
🧪
What are the signs of nutritional deficiency vs. infectious disease in farmed fish?
+
Nutritional deficiencies tend to present gradually and symmetrically: lordosis/scoliosis (vitamin C or phosphorus), cataracts (riboflavin or zinc), fin erosion (pantothenic acid), pale musculature (selenium/vitamin E). Infectious disease more often presents asymmetrically with focal lesions and sudden mortality spikes. Histopathology and feed audit in parallel is the most reliable diagnostic approach.
🥗
Still have a nutrition question?
Our aquaculture nutritionists are available to advise your farm directly.
